chunks.text: Fall 2024 Colloquium - EECS at Berkeley Fall 2024 Colloquium Fall 2024 Dedicated to hosting presentations at the intersection of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science, the EECS Colloquium offers a mix of short talks by faculty describing their latest research results as well as internationally distinguished speakers. It provides an opportunity for an exchange of ideas between our department’s community and the outside world. Wednesdays 4:00-5:00 PM, HP Auditorium, 306 Soda Hall (unless otherwise noted) Light refreshments served at 3:30 pm Subscribe to the eecs-announce mailing list through the web interface or via email so you can be apprised of upcoming talks. Talk recordings will become available four business days after each session. Lars Rasmussen, Tech entrepreneur, software engineer, and angel investor From Theory PhD to Startup Land Youtube Webinar Banatao Auditorium, 310 Sutardja Dai Hall September 4, 2024: 2:00 – 3:00 pm Alexandre Bayen, UC Berkeley Mixed-Autonomy Traffic at Scale Youtube Webinar September 11, 2024 Richard Murray, California Institute of Technology One in a Billion: How to Make Sure Autonomous Systems Are Safe (Enough) Youtube Webinar September 18, 2024: 4:00 – 5:15 pm No talk this week September 25, 2024 Sayeef Salahuddin, UC Berkeley New Pathways for Energy Efficient Computing Hardware Youtube Webinar October 2, 2024: 4:00 – 5:00 pm Tsu-Jae King Liu, UC Berkeley Disruptive Innovation and Collaboration to Sustain the AI Revolution Youtube Webinar Banatao Auditorium, 310 Sutardja Dai Hall October 16, 2024: 2:00 – 3:00 pm Michael I. Jordan, UC Berkeley A Collectivist Vision for AI
